title: Blocks, CAPTCHAs, Rate Signals, Identity, and No-Evasion Boundary · Language Clinic: Block False Positive Review
summary: A 200 challenge page is parsed as content, the team rotates identities, solves CAPTCHAs, hides automation, and treats owner defenses as an engineering obstacle. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the block false positive review increment in block detection and owner-escalation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Test block classification against changed fixtures and require human review before deleting data, escalating, or resuming.
Pause, preserve evidence, contact the owner, request an API or export, negotiate rate and fields, and record changed authorization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-block-false-positive-review — Test block classification against changed fixtures and require human review before deleting data, escalating, or resuming.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-blocks-anti-bot-boundary-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-blocks-anti-bot-boundary-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_blocks_anti_bot_boundary_3(responses: int = 30, normal_pages: int = 24, blocked_pages: int = 6, evasion_attempts: int = 0, owner_escalations: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if responses < 1 or normal_pages < 0 or blocked_pages < 0:
        return False, responses, "block-count"
    if normal_pages + blocked_pages != responses:
        return False, blocked_pages, "block-reconciliation"
    if evasion_attempts > 0:
        return False, evasion_attempts, "evasion-boundary"
    if owner_escalations != blocked_pages:
        return False, owner_escalations, "owner-coordination"
    assert normal_pages + blocked_pages == responses and evasion_attempts == 0
    return True, blocked_pages, "no-evasion-block-policy"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-block-false-positive-review-1pjjno
# Competency: scraper-block-false-positive-review.
# Case 3511: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_block_false_positive_review_1pjjno(responses: int = 30, normal_pages: int = 24, blocked_pages: int = 6, evasion_attempts: int = 0, owner_escalations: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1pjjno = "1pjjno-onjjp1-3511"
    assert evidence_variant_1pjjno
    evidence_axes_1pjjno = (
        "authorization1pjjno",
        "url3511",
        "responseonjjp1",
        "tree1pjjno3511",
        "frontier35111pjjno",
        "reportonjjp13511",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1pjjno)) == 6
    if responses < 1 or normal_pages < 0 or blocked_pages < 0:
        return False, responses, "block-count"
    if normal_pages + blocked_pages != responses:
        return False, blocked_pages, "block-reconciliation"
    if evasion_attempts > 0:
        return False, evasion_attempts, "evasion-boundary"
    if owner_escalations != blocked_pages:
        return False, owner_escalations, "owner-coordination"
    assert normal_pages + blocked_pages == responses and evasion_attempts == 0
    return True, blocked_pages, "no-evasion-block-policy"
